cycle_history.best_hook: Spent the weekend digging into how Fragments actually works and the mechanics are pretty wild. It takes cbBTC and splits it into two separate tokens. You get BTC-Jr which targets about 1.33x exposure, and BTC-Sr which earns yield from the other side. The crazy part is there is no debt involved here. Unlike margin trading or perps, you can't get liquidated because the use is built into the structure, not a loan. It is specifically designed for people who just want to hold Bitcoin for years without babysitting a position. I think this fixes the biggest problem with traditional use products. No funding rates, no daily rebalance decay, and no margin calls. You just swap back to cbBTC whenever you want. @RallyOnChain flagged this early. I'm curious if this actually becomes the standard for long term exposure. Check it out at waitlist.fragments.org

cycle_history.best_hook: Spent last week digging into how use actually works in DeFi and most of it feels like walking on a tightrope. You get the upside but one bad wick wipes you out completely. Fragments by Ampleforth takes a totally different approach. They use tranching to split cbBTC into two tokens. You have the Senior tranche which is stable and earns yield from the use demand. Then you have Bitcoin Junior. It gets the amplified exposure, about 1.33x, without any debt or borrowing. Since there is no loan structure, you can't get liquidated. It is built for holding through cycles, not for active trading. I think the backtests from 2023 to 2024 showing it outperforming raw BTC are interesting, but I'm curious how it handles a prolonged bear market. You can usually swap back to cbBTC which helps. @RallyOnChain flagged this early. It feels like a cleaner way to get exposure without babysitting margin positions. Check the waitlist at fragments.org and let me know if you think this structure actually replaces traditional use.

I'm still not sure how the tranching math works perfectly, but the backtesting looks solid. Traditional leverage products have usually scared me with their liquidation cliffs. I spent the weekend digging into how tranching actually works because I was tired of getting liquidated on margin. The setup by @FragmentsOrg is pretty interesting. They take cbBTC and split it into two separate tokens. You get BTC-Jr which targets about 1.33x exposure, and BTC-Sr which earns yield from the other side.

Spent the weekend digging into how tranching actually works. It basically splits an asset into two parts. You get a junior token for amplified exposure and a senior token for stability. The crazy part is this structure creates leverage without borrowing.

I've been looking for a way to get more BTC exposure without watching charts all day. Most leverage products scare me because of liquidation risk. You mess up once and your position is gone. That's why I'm interested in what @FragmentsOrg is building with Bitcoin Junior.
